rulings.Case Title: GREG MORRIS VS. SAFECO INSURANCE CO. et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, May 12, 2011, line 1, DEFENDANT CRAWFORD AND COMPANY DEMURRER TO 6TH AMENDED COMPLAINT. DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO THE FRAUD AND BAD FAITH CAUSE OF ACTION IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. THE BAD FAITH CAUSE OF ACTION FAILS BECAUSE THE ADMISSIONS IN THE 6TH AMENDED COMPLAINT AND THE FORCED POLICY SHOW THAT THE POLICY WAS SOLEY FOR OPTION ONE'S BENEFIT. THE FRAUD CLAIM FAILS BECAUSE IT LACKS PARTICULARITY AND PLAINTIFF CANNOT ALLEGE A NEXUS BETWEEN THE ALLEGED FRAUD AND HIS HARM. FURTHER, IT IS UNCLEAR HOW PLAINTIFF RELIED. FURTHER, THE INSURANCE CODE REQUIRED THE INSURER TO COOPERATE WITH GOVERNMENT INVESTIGATIONS. AS TO THE 1983 CLAIMS, WHILE DEFENDANTS WERE NOT PART OF THE FEDERAL ACTION, THE DOCTRINE OF COLLATERAL ESTOPPEL PRECLUDES A PARTY TO PRIOR LITIGATION FROM DISPUTING ISSUES DECIDED THEREIN AGAINST HIM, EVEN WHEN THOSE ISSUES BEAR ON DIFFERENT CLAIMS RAISED IN A LATER CASE. SMITH V. EXXON MOBILE OIL CORP. (2007) 153 CAL.APP.4TH 1407, 1414. BECAUSE THE FEDERAL COURT ALREADY RULED THAT PLAINTIFF COULD NOT ALLEGE A 4TH AMENDMENT ILLEGAL SEARCH, HE IS BARRED FROM RAISING IT HERE BASED UPON THE SAME FACTS UNDER THE DOCTRINE OF RES JUDICATA AND COLLATERAL ESTOPPEL. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: TIMBERLY HUGHES, VS. CYRYZ, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, May 12, 2011, line 10, PLAINTIFF TIMBERLY HUGHES MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. PLAINTIFF PRESENTS NEW EVIDENCE TO SUPPORT HER 2ND MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION WHICH COULD HAVE BEEN PROVIDED WHEN THE COURT HEARD HER 1ST MSA. PLAINTIFF FAILED TO EXERCISE DUE DILIGENCE. NY TIMES V. SUP. CRT. (2005) 135 CAL.APP.4TH 206, 212-213. MOREOVER, THERE CONTINUES TO BE TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT AS TO THE ISSUES REGARDING WHETHER PERMITS WERE REQUIRED AND WHETHER DEFENDANT MATERIALLY BREACHED THE TERMS OF THE LEASE. MSA IS DENIED. =(302/LMG)
